held_out_actions.description: Engie acquired Platinum BESS project as part of 6GW Texas portfolio purchased from Belltown Power; project sited near Savoy, TX on US Highway 82 (~17 acre site)
Residents voiced concerns about Fannin County energy facility at public meeting; cited noise, sound, air pollution, and inadequate firefighting capacity for 17-acre lithium battery site
Texas state leaders publicly advocated for Fannin County residents opposing the new battery facility; pressed Engie for compliance and additional safety measures

nodes.description: Issued order to Taaleri Energia to conform to all applicable NFPA standards per Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233 and NFPA 855 (2020 ed). Required: permit application, UL container test results,

DA presented ex parte petition for Temporary Restraining Order. Judge Martin signed TRO (Cause No. 25-00067) against Taaleri Energia North America LLC, BT Amador Storage LLC, Amador BESS Holdings Inc.
Court hearing scheduled for July 24, 2025 to determine whether TRO will be extended or batteries allowed in
Confirmed TRO still in effect. No compliant safety test results received from developer. Groundwork continues but no batteries permitted in county

Press release announcing Van Zandt County Fire Marshal's rejection of Amador BESS documents; letter excerpts state that submitted materials do not satisfy NFPA 855 (2020 ed) and Texas Local Government
Unanimously adopted temporary moratorium freezing all proposed green energy developments (solar farms, wind installations, BESS) in unincorporated areas. Passed after hours of public testimony

Delta County filed Request for Attorney General Opinion (RQ-0634-KP) asking whether Texas counties have express statutory or constitutional authority to impose moratoria on commercial utility-scale so

Passed resolution opposing Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in Gillespie County, citing safety, economic, and environmental concerns. Also passed resolution requiring documentation for Harper BES
As of this date, Peregrine Energy Solutions had not filed any of the required construction documents with Gillespie County. LCRA connection construction continued.

Filed lawsuit in 216th Judicial District Court against Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC and B&CWR Inc. (d/b/a Cactus Construction), seeking temporary and permanent injunction to stop construction of 145

Filed Petition in Intervention in 216th Judicial District Court, joining the residents' lawsuit as a plaintiff opposing the Rogers Draw BESS. Simultaneously filed Response in Opposition to Rogers Draw

Voted to take action on two energy projects. County announced it would file petition of intervention against Peregrine Energy (confirmed the Nov 26 filing). Residents packed meeting to voice concerns.
Issued Stop Work Order to Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC for the Rogers Draw BESS project in Harper, Texas
Court issued Order DENYING Defendant Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC's Rule 91a Motion to Dismiss and Request for Attorneys' Fees; Plaintiffs awarded entitlement to attorneys' fees under TRCP 91a.7

Filed countersuit against Gillespie County in 216th Judicial District Court, claiming the county had initially approved the project and reversed course after citizen pressure. Sought dismissal of coun

Filed Gillespie County's Answer to Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC's Counterclaims in 216th Judicial District Court; argued threatened injury to public safety is "sufficiently likely" given county infr
Agreed temporary injunction signed halting all construction activities on the Rogers Draw BESS project at Harper until final judgment. Trial date to be set

Applied for Special Use Permit (SUP) to build Ochoa Energy Storage (500MW BESS) on approximately 24-acre property at 27051 Highway Blvd, Katy, TX. Holds two ERCOT queue positions (Ochoa I & II) repres
Held "lunch and learn" community session for local residents to provide details on Ochoa project and energy storage industry
Recommended approval of the Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage

Unanimously denied Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage after public hearing with multiple residents voicing opposition. Overrode Planning and Zoning Committee recommendation. Motion by Council
Citizens expressed continued opposition at city council meeting. Mayor Pro Tem Chris Harris cited proximity to schools and residential areas

Filed appeal and application for declaratory order with Texas Public Utility Commission (PUC Docket No. 58859, SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508). Argued City of Katy's zoning actions exceed regulatory aut
Filed response to Ochoa's PUC appeal, arguing city ordinances do not interfere with PUC or ERCOT jurisdiction and do not conflict with state authority under PURA
Filed with PUC stating direct interest in the outcome of the case, asserting city's denial affects grid authority. PUC decision delayed

Issued Draft Preliminary Order in Docket No. 58859 (Appeal of Ochoa Energy Storage LLC from Actions of the City of Katy and Application for Declaratory Order); Commission referred matter to ALJ at SOA

Procedural schedule set: Ochoa direct testimony April 10, 2026; City direct testimony May 15, 2026; Staff and ERCOT testimony June 1, 2026; Cross-rebuttal July 6, 2026; Hearing on the Merits August 4-
Deadline for Ochoa direct testimony filing per adopted procedural schedule (PUC Docket 58859 / SOAH Docket 473-26-07508)
Deadline for City of Katy direct testimony filing per adopted procedural schedule (PUC Docket 58859 / SOAH Docket 473-26-07508)

Proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System (~1,100 acres, 150MW solar + 150MW BESS) near Willow City in Gillespie County, TX. AMPYR described as an Indonesian energy inv
Communicated in writing to AMPYR Energy that the Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance applies to the proposed Marshall Springs project

Sent letter to AMPYR Energy pressing for answers regarding the proposed 1,000-acre BESS and solar facility, citing concerns about Hill Country landscape, drought, wildfire, flash flooding risk, volunt
Passed resolution opposing Solar and BESS projects, specifically targeting the proposed Marshall Springs project. Required Marshall Springs Solar Farm and BESS documentation
Sent formal Notice of Applicability of Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance to the proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System Project

Held information meeting with senior representatives of federal, state, county, and city officials. Attendees included aides to U.S. Senator John Cornyn, U.S. Senator Ted Cruz, U.S. Rep. Chip Roy, Tex
Residents packed commissioners court meeting to voice continued concerns with both the Marshall Springs and Rogers Draw projects
Signed ERCOT interconnection agreement for 150MW Marshall Springs Solar project in Gillespie County, advancing the development despite local opposition
Sent follow-up letter to AMPYR Energy renewing pressure, requesting answers on Chinese components and funding sources, environmental risks, and community impact
Filed lawsuit (Case No. 26-18599) against Marshall Springs Storage LLC, Marshall Springs Solar LLC, and Ampyr Energy USA LLC in Gillespie County District Court
Submitted ERCOT generation interconnection request (queue ID ERCOT-19INR0169) for 250 MW Sun Valley Solar in Hill County, TX
Standard Generation Interconnection Agreement (SGIA) executed for 250 MW Sun Valley Solar at 345 kV Sam Sw – Tradinghouse tap
Long-term Virtual Power Purchase Agreement (VPPA) executed between P&G and ENGIE for output of 250 MW Sun Valley Solar Center
Approved proposed format of Tax Abatement Agreement with Sun Valley Storage, LLC and ordered notice posted on county bulletin board and online (Judge Lewis and all Commissioners voted to approve)

Held public hearing on Hill County's intent to enter into Tax Abatement Agreement with Sun Valley Storage, LLC relating to the solar energy storage project; agenda item included "Discussion and possib
Public announcement of 200 MW PPA from Sun Valley Solar ("located in Hill County, Texas, 65 miles southwest of Dallas"); >530,000 MWh/year to P&G
Sun Valley Solar (250 MW) reached commercial operations and began delivering energy to ERCOT
Filed self-certification of Exempt Wholesale Generator (EWG) status with FERC (Docket No. EG23-140-000) for 100 MW co-located battery storage facility
Issued Notice of Effectiveness of Sun Valley Storage's EWG status (Docket No. EG23-140-000)

Announced commissioning of Sun Valley Battery Storage project (100 MW / 100 MWh, 308 battery cabinets) co-located with the 250 MW Sun Valley Solar plant; ~3,500 workdays on the project. Reaches commer
Plus Power began developing Anemoi Energy Storage project; commenced outreach and engagement with Hidalgo County Planning Department and Fire Marshal's office
Conducted ERCOT interconnectability study for Anemoi Energy Storage
Generation Interconnection Agreement executed for 200 MW BESS adjacent to AEP Pomelo 345 kV substation
Engineering, Procurement & Construction (EPC) contract executed for the 200 MW / 400 MWh BESS
Project site purchase option agreement executed for ~13 acres near Edinburg, TX (south of McCook)
Issued construction-related permit to Anemoi Energy Storage LLC
Issued building permit to Anemoi Energy Storage LLC for 200 MW BESS facility
Phase I Environmental Site Assessment completed for project parcel
Commenced construction of 200 MW / 400 MWh battery energy storage system
Published draft Board Document BD 2023-XX certification and financing proposal for Anemoi project; explicitly states "No public opposition to the Project has been detected"
Filed self-certification of Exempt Wholesale Generator (EWG) status with FERC for the 200 MW battery storage facility under development in Edinburg, Texas
NADBank announced participation in $202.3 million project financing for Anemoi Energy Storage
Unanimously approved 5-year, 50% Tax Abatement Agreement with Anemoi Energy ($190M project value); approved without significant public input or opposition
Anemoi Energy Storage achieved Commercial Operation Date (200 MW / 400 MWh, 2-hour duration); Plus Power reports cleared for operation in June 2024
Informational filing of executed ERCOT Interconnection Agreement with PUCT (Project No. 35077, Item 1810)
Submitted ERCOT generation interconnection request (queue ID ERCOT-23INR0166) for 103.05 MW battery storage project in Hidalgo County, TX
Established SA Grid Solutions joint venture; Great Kiskadee Storage assigned as first project owned under JV
Construction continued in Hidalgo County under previously-secured permits; project initially anticipated COD Q3 2024 (per Apex financing announcement)
Closed >$150M financing for Great Kiskadee Storage and a second Texas BESS project; First Citizens served as sole bookrunner and co-lead arranger; project under construction in Hidalgo County

Issued Public Notice of Meeting at which Commissioners Court would consider approval of a Tax Abatement Agreement for Project Great Kiskadee under Texas Tax Code §312.207; meeting scheduled for June 1
Issued Public Notice of Meeting for Project Great Kiskadee Tax Abatement Agreement; continued/rescheduled consideration
Announced commercial operations of Great Kiskadee Storage (100 MW / 200 MWh, 2-hour duration); first operational asset of SA Grid Solutions JV; equipment provided by Powin
Equinor subsidiary East Point Energy began developing the 250 MW Flat Rock Energy Center on 10 acres at Flat Rock Creek Road, Comfort, Kendall County, TX (adjacent to LCRA substation)

More than 275 residents crowded into Comfort Middle School to voice opposition to East Point's Flat Rock Energy Center and Key Capture Energy's adjacent Ringtrail Ridge BESS (3-acre site on 8-acre pla
Adopted 2024 International Fire Code and implemented restrictive design measures applicable to BESS facilities; effectively raised compliance burden for proposed projects

Kendall County Commissioners Court voted to move toward establishment of a Sub-Regional Planning Commission ("391 Commission") with Kerr County to strengthen joint opposition to BESS development under
Began strengthening Kendall County safety standards for BESS facilities; consulted with nationally-recognized fire/life-safety expert; implemented protective design requirements

Filed multiple Texas state bills (HB 5569, HB 5572, HB 1378, SB 819) to give counties authority to regulate BESS facilities, require fire safety standards, and impose setback rules — explicitly motiva

Issued letter formally cancelling the Flat Rock Energy Center: "Due to shifting strategic priorities, East Point Energy's development of the Flat Rock Energy Center will no longer be pursued at this t
Publicly declared victory: "East Point is officially calling off their plans for a battery storage facility" — framed cancellation as a major victory after fierce local opposition
Engie acquired Platinum BESS project as part of 6GW Texas portfolio purchased from Belltown Power; project sited near Savoy, TX on US Highway 82 (~17 acre site)
Residents voiced concerns about Fannin County energy facility at public meeting; cited noise, sound, air pollution, and inadequate firefighting capacity for 17-acre lithium battery site
Texas state leaders publicly advocated for Fannin County residents opposing the new battery facility; pressed Engie for compliance and additional safety measures
Announced Amador Energy Storage Project (100MW/200MWh BESS) approximately 11 miles southwest of Canton, Van Zandt County, TX
Presented Amador BESS project to Van Zandt County Commissioners Court at workshop meeting

Rejected Black Mountain Energy Storage's request for property tax abatement on $87 million BESS project at Reservation Rd / IH-10 adjacent to Mountain Home substation; eight speakers spoke against the

Signed two formal resolutions opposing BESS in Kerr County: one to be sent to the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ, the BESS-permitting agency); one to be sent to State Rep-elect Wes Vi
Sent letter to Black Mountain Energy Storage CEO Rhett Bennett pressing for answers regarding the proposed Mountain Home BESS facility

Held workshop on forming a Chapter 391 Sub-Regional Planning Commission with Kendall County to formally oppose BESS development across both counties; commissioners unanimously agreed to share the prop
ERCOT generation interconnection request entered queue (ERCOT-25INR0210) for 100.8 MW BESS at "7750 LCRA MOUNTAIN HOME SUB" in Kerr County (queue date 02-01-2025)

Approved hire of contracted (part-time) fire marshal and adopted a new legally vetted resolution requiring BESS developers to prove compliance with state safety and fire codes before receiving court a

Told Lower Colorado River Authority Board of Directors that the 391 SRPC had submitted four coordination notices asking LCRA to consult on proposed BESS sites and that LCRA had refused to meet; called
Black Mountain Energy Storage sold the 200 MW Apache Hill BESS project to woman-owned IPP Vitis Energy

Filed lawsuit (Cause No. 24-00204) seeking damages, temporary and permanent injunction against BT Amador Storage LLC, Maria Amador, Jose Valedelmar Amador, RES Group LLC, Belltown Power Texas Land 2 L
Public meeting held where residents voiced safety concerns including risk of fire and environmental damage from lithium battery explosion
Vitis Energy and Enertis Applus+ advanced Apache Hill BESS through engineering, procurement, and construction milestones for the 200 MW / 400 MWh project at 2625 Mambrino Hwy, Hood County, TX
Vitis Energy announced financial close of Apache Hill (200 MW / 400 MWh); commercial operations targeted for July 2026
Residents urged Hood County Commissioners Court to grant the Hood County Fire Marshal "every tool he needs to inspect these" battery storage facilities; cited proximity to Rainbow's Promise childcare
